# GTLNAV — autogenerated Next.js SSR Dockerfile.
# This template is injected when a Next.js project is detected and no
# Dockerfile is present in the repo. The build worker writes it into
# the cloned repo root before running `docker build`.
#
# Build args:
#   NODE_VERSION   default: 20-alpine
#   PORT           default: 3000

FROM node:${NODE_VERSION}

ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=${PORT}

# Create a non-root user.
RUN addgroup -S gtlnav && adduser -S gtlnav -G gtlnav
WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies first for layer caching.
COPY package.json ./
COPY package-lock.json* ./
COPY pnpm-lock.yaml* ./
COPY yarn.lock* ./
RUN if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then npm i -g pnpm@9 && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile=false; \
    elif [ -f yarn.lock ]; then npm i -g yarn && yarn install; \
    else npm install --no-audit --no-fund; fi

# Copy the rest of the source.
COPY . .

# Build.
RUN if [ -f pnpm-lock.yaml ]; then pnpm run build; \
    elif [ -f yarn.lock ]; then yarn build; \
    else npm run build; fi

USER gtlnav
EXPOSE ${PORT}

CMD ["node", "node_modules/next/dist/bin/next", "start", "-H", "0.0.0.0", "-p", "${PORT}"]
